channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "Violent, Aggressive": China Coast Guard Attack On Filipino Sailor Caught On Camera

media:content.media:description: Chinese coast guard inflatable boat carrying eight personnel deployed from a ship. (Representational)

item.description: The assault was captured on video by other Filipino sailors in the hotly disputed Second Thomas Shoal and set off angry condemnations by Philippine officials.
item.content:encoded: The assault was captured on video by other Filipino sailors in the hotly disputed Second Thomas Shoal and set off angry condemnations by Philippine officials.

item.title: Op Sindoor Big Success, Pak Army Tried To Hide Failure: Imran Khan's Sister

media:content.media:description: Noreen Niazi, sister of Imran Khan, said Pakistani military fabricated a "victory narrative"

item.description: Pakistan's National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan
item.content:encoded: Pakistan's National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has summoned Noreen Niazi, sister of former prime minister Imran Khan

item.title: Iran Is In "Full-Scale War" With US, Says President Pezeshkian

media:content.media:description: In June, inflation in Iran accelerated sharply due to the war

item.description: "The most important battlefield today is the economy and the livelihoods of the people," said the Iranian president in a meeting in Tehran with judicial officials.
item.content:encoded: "The most important battlefield today is the economy and the livelihoods of the people," said the Iranian president in a meeting in Tehran with judicial officials.

item.title: 'Politicians Come And Go': Larry The Cat Welcomes Another UK PM, His 7th

media:content.media:description: Larry the cat, the UK's most famous feline, has been patrolling the corridors of power of 15 years now.

item.description: Recruited to deal with a rodent problem, Larry arrived at the prime minister's official residence on February 15, 2011, when David Cameron was leader.
item.content:encoded: Recruited to deal with a rodent problem, Larry arrived at the prime minister's official residence on February 15, 2011, when David Cameron was leader.

item.title: Khalil Al-Hayya Named New Hamas Chief 2 Years After Yahya Sinwar's Death

media:content.media:description: Hayya defeated Khaled Meshaal in the leadership contest.

item.description: Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike.
item.content:encoded: Born in 1960, Hayya survived an Israeli assassination attempt in Doha last year, though one of his sons was killed in the strike.

item.title: Andy Burnham Becomes UK's 7th Prime Minister In A Decade

media:content.media:description: King Charles Britain's King Charles III shakes hands with Britain's incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham.

item.description: Starmer made a short farewell speech outside the prime minister's 10 Downing Street residence, saying "my work is done."
item.content:encoded: Starmer made a short farewell speech outside the prime minister's 10 Downing Street residence, saying "my work is done."

item.title: US Identifies 2 Soldiers Killed In Iran Strike On Jordan. One Of Them Was 19

media:content.media:description: The US military has said the total number of deaths in the war with Iran is 17.

item.description: On Saturday, the US Central Command said two service members were killed in Jordan while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks.
item.content:encoded: On Saturday, the US Central Command said two service members were killed in Jordan while defending against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks.

item.title: Why Britain Is Getting A New Prime Minister Without A General Election

media:content.media:description: Britain's parliamentary democracy allows governing parties to change leaders midterm.

item.description: Burnham was the only candidate to secure enough support from his fellow Labour lawmakers to replace Starmer as leader of the governing party. Because Labour holds a majority in government, its leader also becomes the UK prime minister.
item.content:encoded: Burnham was the only candidate to secure enough support from his fellow Labour lawmakers to replace Starmer as leader of the governing party. Because Labour holds a majority in government, its leader also becomes the UK prime minister.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Not Just Jets, China Is Developing A '5-Piece' Carrier Set, And India Should Worry
item.description: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.
item.content:encoded: China is now the only state serially producing two distinct stealth fighter families simultaneously, with some estimates projecting that advanced-fighter output across all jet programmes could reach 300-400 aircraft annually by 2027.

item.title: As Europe Hardens Its Line, A Quiet German Back Channel Reaches Russia

media:content.media:description: The German government has publicly distanced itself from these meetings.

item.description: The latest meeting reportedly took place in Baku, Azerbaijan, where former German politicians met representatives linked to Russian President Vladimir Putin's circle.
item.content:encoded: The latest meeting reportedly took place in Baku, Azerbaijan, where former German politicians met representatives linked to Russian President Vladimir Putin's circle.

item.title: Spain's World Cup Win Celebrations Turn Trafic As Teen Dies In Fountain Collapse

media:content.media:description: The fountain collapsed under the weight of numerous people who had climbed onto it

item.description: Overcrowded fountain collapses during midnight soccer celebrations, killing a 13-year-old boy and injuring several others.
item.content:encoded: Overcrowded fountain collapses during midnight soccer celebrations, killing a 13-year-old boy and injuring several others.

item.title: Inside The Secret CIA Mission That Cleared UAE's Path To Advanced AI Chips

media:content.media:description: A senior CIA officer was sent to Abu Dhabi to assess one of the country's most powerful leaders (File)

item.description: Jonny Gannon's mission reportedly went beyond intelligence gathering and became part of a larger effort to reshape US-UAE AI ties.
item.content:encoded: Jonny Gannon's mission reportedly went beyond intelligence gathering and became part of a larger effort to reshape US-UAE AI ties.

item.title: Trump Mum On Iran Plans As US Military Death Count Rises To 17 In War

media:content.media:description: Trump acknowledged the fallen soldiers while speaking to reporters Sunday

item.description: Secret B-2 bomber videos and covert tanker movements hint at a major looming escalation while Trump publicly stalls.
item.content:encoded: Secret B-2 bomber videos and covert tanker movements hint at a major looming escalation while Trump publicly stalls.

item.title: 'Waiting For Their Arrival': Iran's Big Warning Against US Ground Invasion

media:content.media:description: Iran's Foreign Ministry warned that US forces will face the repercussions

item.description: US President Trump has repeatedly mused about ramping up American efforts, including potentially attempting to seize Kharg Island.
item.content:encoded: US President Trump has repeatedly mused about ramping up American efforts, including potentially attempting to seize Kharg Island.

item.title: Indian Envoy Meets 'Distressed' Women Workers In Oman, Assures Help

media:content.media:description: The embassy said it provides free boarding and lodging facilities to distressed workers.

item.description: The embassy's outreach comes amid recurring reports of abuse, exploitation and non-payment of wages involving Indian women domestic workers in Oman and other Gulf countries.
item.content:encoded: The embassy's outreach comes amid recurring reports of abuse, exploitation and non-payment of wages involving Indian women domestic workers in Oman and other Gulf countries.

item.title: Team Trump Explores New Deportation Path In 'Alien Terrorist Removal' Court

media:content.media:description: The proceedings under the court are set in two stages: one private and the other public.

item.description: If the Trump administration wins this case, it could set up a new precedent of removing people it links to terrorism while keeping much of its evidence secret.
item.content:encoded: If the Trump administration wins this case, it could set up a new precedent of removing people it links to terrorism while keeping much of its evidence secret.

item.title: New Air Force One To Be "Maxed Out" Amid Security Concerns: Donald Trump

media:content.media:description: Trump took his first flight on the retrofitted Boeing 747-8 plane on July 1.

item.description: President Trump grounds vulnerable new Qatari-gifted jet for a month-long security overhaul following anti-missile defense gaps.
item.content:encoded: President Trump grounds vulnerable new Qatari-gifted jet for a month-long security overhaul following anti-missile defense gaps.

item.title: 200 Made-In-India Coaches To Give Boost To Bangladesh Railways

media:content.media:description: Bangladesh Railway will receive 200 new coaches from India.

item.description: Bangladesh will import 200 broad-gauge railway coaches from India by December 2027 under a Rs 915 crore deal.
item.content:encoded: Bangladesh will import 200 broad-gauge railway coaches from India by December 2027 under a Rs 915 crore deal.

item.title: Canada Defies UN Advice, Bans Visitors From Congo Amid Ebola Outbreak

media:content.media:description: The UN health agency said travel restrictions create stigma

item.description: The ban contravenes advice from the World Health Organization, which advises against travel or trade restrictions with Congo, where officials are currently struggling to contain a large outbreak of Ebola.
item.content:encoded: The ban contravenes advice from the World Health Organization, which advises against travel or trade restrictions with Congo, where officials are currently struggling to contain a large outbreak of Ebola.

item.title: Tate Brothers Arrested On Rape, Trafficking Charges: A Look At Their Controversies

media:content.media:description: Tate brothers are facing several criminal investigations in the UK and Romania

item.description: Andrew Tate, 39, now faces 42 charges, while Tristan Tate, 38, faces 17.
item.content:encoded: Andrew Tate, 39, now faces 42 charges, while Tristan Tate, 38, faces 17.
